specific_location: Elk Thistle Dr. in the Wildernest subdivision
description: Small, pale finches described here as one, though with comments about differences in sexes/ages; with yellow bill; variably bright red crown patch separated from black above bill by white or whitish forehead (barely minimal in some individuals); white superciliary; streaked gray-white auriculars; grayish-brown rear crown and nape streaked darker; chests and sides variably pink (in males) or white (in females and possibly some imm. males); sides with obvious dark brownish-gray streaks; white belly, vent, and undertail coverts, with many of latter with dark shaft streaksBehaviors: attending feedersCall: chittery flight calls like those of Pine Siskin, but more run togetherPlumage: Formative and/or basic
